transcript.text: Hey, Jason.
How you doing?
Not too bad.
Happy Friday.
Yeah, that's always a good thing.
Any plans this weekend?
No, I'm going to just watch football, relax, take it easy.
Nice.
How about you?
Gosh, no, not really much.
My son has a soccer game, and that's about it, but I'll probably be watching football myself.
What teams do you watch?
I'm in fantasy, so I watch every team now, but I'm a Chiefs fan.
Oh, really?
Okay.
Are you from that area?
I grew up there for a short stint and went to my first NFL game there.
So I've been a Chiefs fan before Mahomes kind of came as our savior.
I was just going to make the joke.
Hi, by the way.
I was going to make the joke that he's just a huge Taylor Swift fan.
That's my secret.
That's my secret.
They don't have to be mutually exclusive.
Yeah, that's funny.
Good morning.
Yeah, absolutely.
Good morning.
Appreciate you guys taking the time to do this.
So we typically run through these, and I guess for larger context, a lot of our clients aren't a B2C model.
They're more of a B2B.
And so we kind of have like sales demos to walk through, but I'm not sure if there's a way for you to share a mobile app screen or if we just want to talk through kind of product details.
I was imagining just walking you through the desktop app, which I can certainly share.
It's pretty much the exact same version.
I can even narrow the screen if the mobile view matters to you, but that might be just easiest.
Yeah, that works.
Mobile view does not matter.
why don't I – one of the things I like to do is analyze questions people ask to try to figure out conceptions, preconceptions.
And so, Erik, by your question, I'm guessing you're thinking that it's mainly an app.
And that's why I want to share is that auto in general, we think of it just as the best ever executive assistant to help you book your travel.
And so if you think about the model of an executive assistant, how do you talk to your executive assistant?
Or if you ever talk to an office manager who's helping you out or something like that.
And the answer is yes, right?
There is no one way.
And so what we designed auto to be is completely independent of the form factor or the mechanism by which to talk to auto.
And so you definitely have an iOS app.
You have an Android app if you prefer that.
There is a website that you can go to, and that's what Jason is going to take you through.
You can email auto, you know, in the very near future.
And this is more just a decision of taking a couple of weeks to add it in, the infrastructure and the architecture is all set up for it.
You can Slack auto and ask auto questions.
You can use Teams to contact auto.
You can send auto an SMS or a WhatsApp or an iMessage.
And then at some point in the future, you know, you pick up the phone and you call auto and you can talk to auto.
And so think of it more as auto is a conversational agent that helps you book your travel.
There's definitely some benefits when you have a web experience.
And again, Jason will take you to this where you can show more visuals.
You can show the logos of the airlines.
You know, people are visual people and they see things that way.
But that's something we take advantage of rather than that's the necessity of the product, if that makes sense.
Yeah, absolutely.
And just a quick follow before we jump in.
And so we're kind of honing.
And again, on personas, I know road warriors are kind of like top of mind, but would executive assistants themselves be able to use this for like multiple, like if they manage multiple executives?
Yeah, we haven't added just for, I mean, this is more of a product decision for now.
We haven't added the ability to impersonate the wrong word, to essentially be a second user on someone else's account.
And so for now, that's not possible, but that's absolutely something that we're going to and will add.

And so essentially, know, Otto will know that you are Erik O'Brien, but Otto will also know that you can come in and say, hey, Otto, I'm booking travel for Jason Flateboe, and then Otto will be able to know that you have a relationship.
With Jason's account, and we'll go pull all of Jason's preferences, all of those things.

So essentially, you're working within your account, and you're talking to Otto, no different than if you were an executive assistant, and you'd call someone and say, hey, I'm booking travel for my boss.
But, you know, so as you can kind of hear by how I'm talking, that spec is built.
That spec is well thought out.
It's just a decision of, you know, we just haven't added it yet, because we're trying to narrow that go-to-market delivery.
Yep, absolutely.
Yeah, it was just something that we had kind of teed up as a potential persona, like the EA for an EA.
But for now, we'll kind of remove that, and focus on more of the road warrior kind of executives.
And Eric, expect this to be a two-way street as well.
You know, if I jump fully into the GrowthX story that was shared with me initially by Marcel, is that, you know...
Growthx becomes our product marketing and product content marketing team.
Think of it as an in-house group.
Then this is a two-way street.

If you guys are going through and doing your research and you're seeing that there's a huge opportunity in the market for targeting that persona of the executive assistant, no different than if you were sitting in our office and you're working directly for our team, I'd expect you to say, hey, Michael, there's an opportunity here.
If we build that in, that's a whole different segment that I can target.
Think about that and let us know if you guys start uncovering some potential target audiences that we just haven't done yet.
Yeah.
Yeah, absolutely.
I mean, I'm thinking of it very selfishly for ourselves.
know, startups with multiple people that need to manage kind of travel.

actually not the first time it's happened this week.

was in Chicago for the last 15 years.

I know you guys have probably already looked through some of these screens, but this is where people are going to be coming from.
And then when they land, obviously I am a user who already has used auto, so you would not see this.
But very quickly, we kind of talked through this.
What auto does when you get on is it's learning very quickly about your home airport, your preferences in terms of brands for both flights, hotels, what times you like to travel.
We've kind of gone through all that stuff, but right away, you can start interacting with auto to start planning a trip.

Click a button, and it's an example of, hey, want to go to Florida next Thursday, and right away, otter will just start bringing back flights.

So, otter's going to start bringing back flights for me, it'll also, as I start picking my flights and making selections, it'll go into a hotel selection process, but it's basically going to plan my whole trip out around, not only these dates, but the preferences, and this is kind of a good example right here.
Otter's telling me what it knows about me already, based on...
And some travel preferences that have been collected on my previous flight.
So as I'm using this more and more, Otto knows me better and better and is giving me more tailored trip selections.
So here's my Alaska flights.
I can see other parts.
Let me just pause you on that, Jason.
And, Erik, this is a big thing and probably one of the biggest differentiators and something I would expect quite a bit of content on if I was just writing it.
It is really two different concepts.
The first is that Otto learns from your interactions and gets better with every single experience.
You'll hear me keep coming back to the concept of the best ever executive assistant because that's really what every single time a question comes up internally with the engineering team, with design.
Whatever question it is, I'll come back with, well, what would the best ever executive assistant do?
Okay.
And so as you're working with an EA, then you say, hey, I really like flying Delta, but actually really what the main thing I want is a nonstop flight, things like that.
Or what you just do is your EA presents you with a bunch of hotel options.
And then after you've basically picked the Bonvoy or Marriott hotel three times in a row, then your EA kind of goes, aha, I think I figured out this person really likes Marriott hotels.
And so auto will do the same thing.
It's learning as you go along.
And so it's getting a better and better understanding of, you know, does this person really like flying in the mornings?
There's a person like flying in the evenings.
And the great thing is we haven't set up a, you know, kind of a, your typical key variable type of a thing where here are the 10 things that auto learns.
It literally learned down to nuances of, you know.
I think this person always likes sitting on the right-hand side of the plane by the window when they're flying east.
Without us telling Otto, that's something to go look for.
And so this is part of that learning.
And when Jason says, oh, it knows that, you know, I don't want red-eye flights.
I prefer the front of the plane.
You can read there.
Obviously, the Alaska Airlines was in the actual request.
But those are the types of things that Otto learns.

And so what we really are calling that, what I'm calling that to lots of folks is, you know, this is the first of, I think, a breed of apps and applications and usages that are really going into hyper-personalization, right?
This isn't just personalization of, you know, the basics of it remembers your name, it remembers your login, and, you know, your frequent flyer number, things like that.
It'll do all that, but it's really hyper-personalization in the sense that it'll start personalizing based on things.
No one ever taught it to personalize on.
No one ever instructed it to personalize on these things, down to the level of hotels.
So we had a user the other day who very excitedly told me, and I kind of said, yeah, that's what I would expect.
He very excitedly told me that he was like, yeah, you know, I know I've used auto to go to Philadelphia three times.
And if I'm correct, I think it's actually Century City, which is a little town or a city outside of Philadelphia.
He said, I searched for Philadelphia hotels, and because I end up always booking in Century City, it's actually now figured out to find me hotels in Century City.
And he goes, the first option, the one I was recommending was the hotel I've stayed at before.
I'm like, yep, that's how it would work, right?
That's what the best of executive assistant would do.
And that's that level of personalization where your auto by itself starts narrowing in and finding that particular hotel because it knows.
That's the hotel.
But even beyond that, you say, I want to go to Chicago.

Well, actually, by Chicago, maybe this user actually means they want to go to Highland Park specifically because they're visiting a company and Otto knows that the headquarters of that company is out in Highland Park.
And it's that type of thing.
So pair the self-learning and preferences with that hyper-personalization and start getting this experience.
And just to add to that personalization story that Michael's talking about, think there's another kind of angle to this, too, where Otto knows your travel policy for your company, right?
So you can copy-paste or upload that, and Otto will understand that you can only spend $300 a night on a hotel or, like, in this case, only refundable flights, right?
So although there's a $150 Sabre fare, I can see these other fares if I want, but Otto knows that...
The one that it should recommend is this $300 refundable because that's really all I'm ever going to buy.
So it's saving me a step of like filtering through a bunch of different fares and just cutting to like what my travel policy is really calling for.
And you can actually, this is made up, but there's actually a place where you can go interact with the auto and tell it, you know, like I said, copy, paste your travel policy.
You can modify it.
I could say, hey, when I go to New York, San Francisco, or LA, actually get to spend $100 more per night on hotels and like it'll learn all those rules and figure that out.
Any other, because I know that you're a current user, any other kind of preference center or preference like questions that are asked on like a new user?
Before they start jumping into the experience.
It's so it's not.
And it's not patterned necessarily.
It's sort of like the questions vary on how you're interacting with Otto.
And it's learning sort of behind the scenes as you go as well.
So like I think Michael mentioned earlier, like we used to have an onboarding flow where it was like, hey, what's your favorite airline?
What's your favorite this?
What's your favorite that?
And we moved away from that into more of just an organic collection and like over time learning about the user.
But Otto does ask, like, you know, if it's not sure, you know, it's sort of like, I think the design principle is more like ask at the right time to clarify and learn.
Anything to add to that, Michael?

So, if you're already, you know, maybe you're halfway through your trip or you're just about to take your trip and something happens, your flight gets changed or canceled, like, we won't.
How to handle those scenarios as well?
Yeah, so booking a flight and booking a hotel is kind of the boring first bit.
People have been able to do that online for a long time.
Servicing a trip, especially for business travelers, is actually one of the biggest pain points.
I remember from working at Egencia, which is one of the big corporate travel companies that's now a part of Amex GBT.
One of the things there was that when something goes wrong or a user needs to change something, 80% to 90% of people call in.
They're just not able to do it online, and the online systems aren't set up for it.
With auto, you can just go to auto and say, hey, I'm not going to be able to make my flight back.
Move it to the next day.
Auto will confirm the change of your flight to the next day.
And then realize that you have a hotel room booked as well.
And then we'll ask if it needs to extend your hotel room.
You say yes.
And so little things like that, it's the servicing aspect that's one of the biggest pain points.
People have to spend hours and hours on the phone with either the airline, the hotel, or call in to whatever travel company they booked it through.
And now with auto, you can just ask auto, and they'll handle things for you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Product deep dive into Otto, an AI-powered travel booking assistant for business travelers.](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=71.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Otto is a conversational AI travel assistant accessible via multiple platforms (web, mobile, email, Slack, etc.)](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=165.0)
  - [Offers hyper-personalized travel recommendations and bookings based on user preferences and company policies](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=204.0)
  - [Simplifies the booking process with natural language interactions and efficient servicing capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=232.0)
  - [Currently focuses on flights and hotels for individual business travelers, with plans to expand features](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=330.0)

## Topics

### Otto's Core Functionality

  - [Acts as an AI-powered executive assistant for travel booking](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=68.0)
  - [Accessible through multiple channels (web, mobile, email, Slack, SMS, etc.)](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=165.0)
  - [Learns user preferences over time for hyper-personalized recommendations](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=756.0)
  - [Simplifies booking process with conversational interface and natural language understanding](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=232.0)
  - [Handles complex multi-city trip planning and itinerary management](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1641.0)

### User Experience and Interface

  - [Web interface demonstrated, similar to mobile app experience](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=500.0)
  - [Users can interact via text or voice input (voice output coming soon)](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1539.0)
  - [No rigid booking flow; users can navigate freely between steps](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1018.0)
  - [Visual elements (e.g., airline logos) enhance the experience when available](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=219.0)
  - [Saves trip progress, allowing users to resume booking later](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1692.0)

### Key Features

  - [Integrates company travel policies, highlighting in-policy vs. out-of-policy options](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1431.0)
  - [Provides detailed flight and hotel recommendations with explanations](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=656.0)
  - [Handles mid-trip changes and servicing requests conversationally](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1923.0)
  - [Offers efficient rebooking and cancellation capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1777.0)
  - [Generates travel receipts formatted for easy expense management import](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1527.0)

### Technical Capabilities

  - [Uses AI to understand complex travel requests and preferences](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=232.0)
  - [Connects to multiple travel inventory sources for comprehensive options](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=82.0)
  - [Implements voice recognition (likely using Whisper API) for speech input](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1539.0)
  - [Plans to add voice output capabilities in the future](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1559.0)

### Current Limitations

  - [Focused on individual business travelers (no multi-traveler bookings yet)](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=331.0)
  - [Limited to flights and hotels (no car rentals, cruises, or restaurants currently)](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1487.0)
  - [No built-in expense management (but provides formatted receipts)](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1507.0)
  - [Lacks approval routing for out-of-policy bookings](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1361.0)
  - [Does not offer flexible date/destination searches for leisure travel](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1597.0)

### Competitive Landscape

  - [Few direct AI-powered travel booking competitors currently operational](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=2724.0)
  - [Main competition from traditional corporate travel management companies (e.g., Amex GBT, Concur)](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=2070.0)
  - [Some startups (e.g., Biztrip.ai, Skylink) in early stages or pivoting](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=2810.0)
  - [Legacy players likely developing AI solutions but facing challenges with existing systems](https://fathom.video/share/9UgTdHNqQTJ-8qkiDDXy7ss2QXUUDe-Y?tab=summary&timestamp=2798.0)

## Next Steps
